ocr.text: The increasing volume of scholarly articles indexed in Scopus over recent years underscores the dynamic growth of academic
research across various disciplines.
Articles indexed in Scopus  2008  2009  2010  2011
In full immediate OA journalsb  118,751  133,817  163,670  181,706
Share of articles published in full immediate OA journals  8.1%  8.6%  10.3%  11%
Articles indexed in Web of Knowledge  2008  2009  2010  2011
In full immediate OA journalsb  76,537  85,852  103,514  116,192
Share of articles published in full immediate OA journals  6.6%  7.1%  8.4%  9.0%
As illustrated in the accompanying table, the total number of articles indexed in Scopus has seen a steady upward trajectory,
rising from 1,469,286 in 2008 to 1,658,643 by 2011. This growth reflects the expanding engagement of researchers and
institutions in disseminating their findings through reputable databases, which are essential for enhancing visibility and

accessibility of academic work. 

Moreover, the year-on-year increase, particularly the jump from 1,550,413 articles in 2009 to
1,588,636 in 2010, emphasizes a significant surge in research output. Such increases can be attributed to various factors,
including advancements in technology, greater collaboration across borders, and the proliferation of open access journals, which
collectively facilitate the rapid dissemination of knowledge. Understanding these trends is crucial for stakeholders in the
academic community, as it not only highlights the evolving landscape of research productivity but also informs decisions
regarding funding, publication strategies, and academic collaborations moving forward.
